How many times did you have to iterate on that?
Take it away, Joey.
Yeah, I think a good question and another really good topic for us to take a leadership position on, because we're doing this different than most other people.

So we exclusively tried to source, basically used a vertically integrated flavor partner, which we can just kind of say is us, where we only source things directly from farm and took, like, all natural essences from flavors that we were, like, targeting.
What we tried to do is provide something that was, I don't know what the language we want to use is, but here's what we actually did.

We went with something where we have a, the kind of formula we went after was like a basic approachable flavor for the masses, combined with something a little bit more adventurous, and so you'll see flavors like Passion Fruit Blackcurrant, maybe that's a little adventurous for both, but like Passion Fruit seems to be something from our consumer insights that like, everybody wants, and, and, Blackcurrant is something like a little more exotic, and kind of connotates something a little bit different, and you can see elements like elderflower, lemonade, same kind of thing, where we look at what people are wanting, kind of trend-based data, like I don't know if you guys have seen, but the Hugo Spritz is like the drink of the summer, which is kind of awesome timing for us, because it's an elderflower-based version of, of the Aperol Spritz, and we can kind of photograph ours with, with, with that a some, important to back

And things like that to make it happen, but this is the kind of, because we're targeting the consumer that we're targeting, they're going to have a little bit of a different taste preference than what you would find from an ultra-mass product that might be like, you know, strawberry or raspberry as the basic formulation.
So we tried to kind of be a little bit more expansive on that.
Iteration-wise and R&D-wise, we did tons of different trials and we sampled, I don't know, probably like 50 different flavors.
And then when you think about the combinations of those flavors that we did, it went much more expansive beyond that.
And then also, what we were trying to accomplish was get this beautiful flavor without having sugar.
And that's an increased level of difficulty for that challenge.
So we now have a product that...

It doesn't have high-intensity sweeteners and also doesn't have any sugar, but still has the freshness and the beautiful taste directly from the farm to give a flavor profile that we think is pretty sophisticated and matches the kind of preferences that our consumers are after.
Okay, yeah, that's super interesting to hear.

It kind of ties into what I wanted to talk about next, which is, I mean, I know the founding team, like your, Joey, your wife, she was, you know, she experienced this as well, like trying out different supplements and things like that.
And I would be curious just to hear, like, what was, or what did you find is lacking in your, like, major competitors?

I know, like from watching your customer videos, it's, you know, some people that don't want to take pills, others find the powders gross, so they don't to drink them, or they find it difficult to, like, form a habit around taking these supplements.
Like, you could just tell me what kind of, what are the areas where your competitors really kind of drop the ball?
Yeah.
Okay, so I would say a few things on that.
One is the form factor that we've used, similar to other powders, is we were able to formulate it in a way that has probably the highest player absorption and bioavailability for people.
And Maggie can kind of help with some specific language for that, but that was one aspect that is attractive.
So we wanted that, so we went with a powder, but then we also decided to go with an effervescent product.
And we felt like that was, again, back to that kind of like sophisticated flavor profile.
Something that if it has a little fizz and it has a little bit of an exotic flavor profile from a natural source, that's something that's going to be really quite nice to take every single day.
And the most common reasons we found that people...
Stop taking their supplementation regimen is because it tastes gross or because of the pill fatigue.
So those are the two things that we really try to avoid.

And when you combine that with something Brian said earlier on like a greens juice as an example, it's pretty gross tasting and people tend to stop taking it because it tastes bad, but also who knows if it works because they've thrown 500 those ingredients at it where we've tried to be much more surgical about exactly what you need based on where you are.
That's kind of a combo there.
The other thing is like gummies are extremely popular.
Like they're almost a quarter of the VMS business now and they cannot formulate specific ingredients into gummies in reasonable concentrations.
Like it's very difficult to get iron as an example into gummies.
And iron is a really critical element for both prenatal as
as as your primary stage when you're shedding a lot of iron through your menstrual cycle.
So we've been able to put all of those ingredients in because we chose this form factor.
And then also we had no sugar and not like a candy, sickly, sweet flavor as well that you'll find with gummies that are sugar-free.
So those are, I'd say those are the, those are probably the big differentiators from form factor on why we tried to do this.
And we feel like we threaded the needle and found like that sweet spot where it tastes really good, very bioavailable.
We're able to put in all the ingredients that we wanted to.
There's, there's almost no exceptions to that.
Um, and yeah, I think that's a good summary.

And what, one last thing, a negative flag is that one of the things that we, we did have to compromise on is that you see, um, quite a bit of settling or precipitation of some materials out of the, uh, of the, like out of solution when you're stirring it in your glass.

## Meeting Purpose

[Deep dive into Biologica's perimenopause product formulation, positioning, and content strategy.](https://fathom.video/share/KFAPVstRz6z5jp-_Yf-dkz9CJ1Zy-VKJ?tab=summary&timestamp=0.0)

## Key Takeaways

  - [Biologica uses premium, clinically-studied ingredients tailored to specific hormonal stages, focusing on bioavailability and efficacy](https://fathom.video/share/KFAPVstRz6z5jp-_Yf-dkz9CJ1Zy-VKJ?tab=summary&timestamp=304.0)
  - [Product differentiators: effervescent form factor, sophisticated flavor profile, no sugar, high bioavailability](https://fathom.video/share/KFAPVstRz6z5jp-_Yf-dkz9CJ1Zy-VKJ?tab=summary&timestamp=1814.0)
  - [Content strategy should focus on educating about hormonal stages, normalizing experiences, and positioning Biologica as a "co-pilot" for hormones](https://fathom.video/share/KFAPVstRz6z5jp-_Yf-dkz9CJ1Zy-VKJ?tab=summary&timestamp=3340.0)
  - [Brand imagery will feature high-end black and white photography, balancing luxury look with accessible pricing](https://fathom.video/share/KFAPVstRz6z5jp-_Yf-dkz9CJ1Zy-VKJ?tab=summary&timestamp=3660.0)

## Topics

### Product Formulation

  - [5-6 trademarked ingredients per formula, tailored to each hormonal stage](https://fathom.video/share/KFAPVstRz6z5jp-_Yf-dkz9CJ1Zy-VKJ?tab=summary&timestamp=304.0)
  - [Focus on bioavailable vitamins and minerals for optimal absorption](https://fathom.video/share/KFAPVstRz6z5jp-_Yf-dkz9CJ1Zy-VKJ?tab=summary&timestamp=361.0)
  - [Clinically-studied probiotic included in each product](https://fathom.video/share/KFAPVstRz6z5jp-_Yf-dkz9CJ1Zy-VKJ?tab=summary&timestamp=384.0)
  - [Formulated without sugar, using natural flavors for a sophisticated taste profile](https://fathom.video/share/KFAPVstRz6z5jp-_Yf-dkz9CJ1Zy-VKJ?tab=summary&timestamp=304.0)

### Competitive Differentiators

  - [Effervescent form factor for increased bioavailability and enjoyable consumption](https://fathom.video/share/KFAPVstRz6z5jp-_Yf-dkz9CJ1Zy-VKJ?tab=summary&timestamp=1814.0)
  - [Curated approach vs. "kitchen sink" formulations of competitors](https://fathom.video/share/KFAPVstRz6z5jp-_Yf-dkz9CJ1Zy-VKJ?tab=summary&timestamp=640.0)
  - [Addresses psycho-social-emotional symptoms (focus, mood, sleep) rather than just physical symptoms](https://fathom.video/share/KFAPVstRz6z5jp-_Yf-dkz9CJ1Zy-VKJ?tab=summary&timestamp=480.0)
  - [No sugar, but maintains a pleasant sweetness profile](https://fathom.video/share/KFAPVstRz6z5jp-_Yf-dkz9CJ1Zy-VKJ?tab=summary&timestamp=1435.0)
  - [Ability to include critical ingredients like iron, which are difficult to formulate in gummies](https://fathom.video/share/KFAPVstRz6z5jp-_Yf-dkz9CJ1Zy-VKJ?tab=summary&timestamp=1147.0)

### Target Audience and Positioning

  - [Women with household incomes over $100,000](https://fathom.video/share/KFAPVstRz6z5jp-_Yf-dkz9CJ1Zy-VKJ?tab=summary&timestamp=271.0)
  - [Positioning as a "co-pilot" for hormones, normalizing the experience rather than treating it as an imbalance](https://fathom.video/share/KFAPVstRz6z5jp-_Yf-dkz9CJ1Zy-VKJ?tab=summary&timestamp=3348.0)
  - [Sophisticated flavor profiles (e.g., Passion Fruit Blackcurrant, Elderflower Lemonade) aligned with target consumer preferences](https://fathom.video/share/KFAPVstRz6z5jp-_Yf-dkz9CJ1Zy-VKJ?tab=summary&timestamp=1814.0)

### Content Strategy Insights

  - [Educate on the five hormonal life stages and their impacts](https://fathom.video/share/KFAPVstRz6z5jp-_Yf-dkz9CJ1Zy-VKJ?tab=summary&timestamp=250.0)
  - [Address common perimenopause symptoms: dulled mental acuity, irritability, mood swings, anxiety, sleep issues](https://fathom.video/share/KFAPVstRz6z5jp-_Yf-dkz9CJ1Zy-VKJ?tab=summary&timestamp=480.0)
  - [Highlight the multi-generational aspect, sharing wisdom from older women to younger generations](https://fathom.video/share/KFAPVstRz6z5jp-_Yf-dkz9CJ1Zy-VKJ?tab=summary&timestamp=3340.0)
  - [Focus on transparency in ingredient sourcing and testing, without overemphasizing provenance](https://fathom.video/share/KFAPVstRz6z5jp-_Yf-dkz9CJ1Zy-VKJ?tab=summary&timestamp=2708.0)

### Brand Imagery

  - [High-end black and white photography shot on real film](https://fathom.video/share/KFAPVstRz6z5jp-_Yf-dkz9CJ1Zy-VKJ?tab=summary&timestamp=3660.0)
  - [Balancing luxury aesthetic with accessible pricing (comparable to H\&M's visual approach)](https://fathom.video/share/KFAPVstRz6z5jp-_Yf-dkz9CJ1Zy-VKJ?tab=summary&timestamp=3689.0)
  - [Mix of hero lifestyle shoots and potentially more budget-friendly content imagery](https://fathom.video/share/KFAPVstRz6z5jp-_Yf-dkz9CJ1Zy-VKJ?tab=summary&timestamp=3660.0)

### Manufacturing and Quality

  - [Adherence to Good Manufacturing Practices (GMP)](https://fathom.video/share/KFAPVstRz6z5jp-_Yf-dkz9CJ1Zy-VKJ?tab=summary&timestamp=1609.0)
  - [Focus on bioavailability, meeting clinical dosages, and purity testing](https://fathom.video/share/KFAPVstRz6z5jp-_Yf-dkz9CJ1Zy-VKJ?tab=summary&timestamp=304.0)
  - [Non-GMO ingredients](https://fathom.video/share/KFAPVstRz6z5jp-_Yf-dkz9CJ1Zy-VKJ?tab=summary&timestamp=304.0)
  - [Third-party testing for purity and potency](https://fathom.video/share/KFAPVstRz6z5jp-_Yf-dkz9CJ1Zy-VKJ?tab=summary&timestamp=1529.0)

## Next Steps

  - [Maggie to provide GMP explanation document](https://fathom.video/share/KFAPVstRz6z5jp-_Yf-dkz9CJ1Zy-VKJ?tab=summary&timestamp=1609.0)
